title: phaseolus vulgaris var. humilis
intro: Phaseolus vulgaris var. humilis, also known as the common wild bean or climbing bean, is a type of vining legume that belongs to the Phaseolus genus. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Phaseolus vulgaris var. humilis include its ability to support digestive health, lower cholesterol levels, and reduce blood sugar levels, making it a valuable herb for managing various health conditions. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
